scenarios.title: POST /invoke, end to end
scenarios.summary: A generic workflow client calls the batch contract. Config is parsed, tools/MCP/secrets are resolved, the runner runs one cold turn through Pi, and the service returns one assistant message with usage recorded on the workflow span.

steps.title: Client sends the batch request
steps.description: A generic workflow client (not necessarily a browser) POSTs /invoke with chat history and the agent config, including the run-selection fields.

agent.agents_md: You are a helpful research assistant.

steps.title: Route dispatches to the agent handler
steps.description: The generic workflow route negotiates batch vs. stream from the Accept header and calls _agent in services/oss/src/agent/app.py.

steps.title: Parse one AgentConfig
steps.description: AgentConfig.from_params coerces the loose payload into the neutral runtime config, including harness, sandbox, and permission_default.

payload.instructions: You are a helpful research assistant.

steps.title: Resolve tools and secrets
steps.description: The service calls resolve_tools and resolve_mcp_servers. The builtin web_search tool passes straight through with no network call; a code or gateway tool would trigger a resolver HTTP call here.

steps.title: Build the /run request and call the runner
steps.description: The service builds SessionConfig, resolves the model connection's secret, and sends one AgentRunRequest to the sidecar over HTTP (or CLI subprocess in a source checkout).

payload.agentsMd: You are a helpful research assistant.

steps.title: Cold start: daemon + pi-acp + Pi CLI
steps.description: The runner has no warm process to reuse. It starts the sandbox-agent daemon, which resolves harness "pi_core" to the ACP agent "pi" and spawns pi-acp, which spawns the pi CLI. Pi loads the bundled extension.

steps.title: Pi runs the turn
steps.description: Pi builds its system prompt (AGENTS.md wraps as <project_context>), runs the model loop, and (if it calls web_search) executes the tool through its extension's registered handler.

payload.text: The capital of Italy is Rome.

steps.title: Runner returns the result
steps.description: One JSON AgentRunResult comes back, including usage read from the Pi extension's writeback file.

payload.output: The capital of Italy is Rome.

steps.title: Teardown, usage rollup, and response
steps.description: The cold session is torn down. Usage is stamped on the live workflow span (not the response body), and the handler returns one assistant message.

outputs.content: The capital of Italy is Rome.

scenarios.title: POST /messages, browser chat streaming
scenarios.summary: The browser opens a streaming chat turn. session_id is minted, UIMessage history is folded into neutral messages, the runner streams NDJSON events internally, and the Vercel adapter projects them into an SSE-framed UI Message Stream.

steps.title: Browser sends a streaming chat turn
steps.description: useChat POSTs /messages with Accept: text/event-stream, no session_id yet (first turn), and Vercel UIMessage history.

steps.title: Mint session_id, validate, negotiate stream
steps.description: session_id is absent, so the route mints one with a sess_ prefix. data.stream is set from the Accept header, not a caller field.

steps.title: Fold UIMessage[] into neutral messages
steps.description: Each UIMessage part becomes a neutral ContentBlock; a text part becomes {type:"text"}.

steps.title: Runner call with NDJSON streaming
steps.description: The same /run request as the batch path, but sent with Accept: application/x-ndjson (or the CLI --stream flag).

steps.title: Runner emits live NDJSON records
steps.description: One JSON object per line, flushed as each event is built: message lifecycle, then a terminal result.

result.output: The last deploy shipped the runner fix.

steps.title: Project AgentEvent objects to Vercel stream parts
steps.description: message_start/delta/end map to text-start/text-delta/text-end; usage rides messageMetadata.usage on finish; done maps to finish-step then finish.

steps.title: Frame as SSE and terminate
steps.description: Each part is sent as an `data: {...}` SSE line; the stream ends with `data: [DONE]`.

scenarios.title: Gateway tool call (Composio, local sandbox)
scenarios.summary: An author-declared gateway tool (a Composio GitHub action) is resolved server-side, delivered to Pi natively through the bundled extension, and executed by posting back to Agenta's /tools/call so the connection's auth never enters the sandbox.

steps.title: Resolve the declared gateway tool
steps.description: The author's config names a Composio action on a connected account. AgentaGatewayToolResolver posts the reference to POST /tools/resolve.

steps.title: API validates the connection and enriches from the catalog
steps.description: The API checks the named connection exists, is active, and is authenticated, then returns a call_ref slug plus the tool's real description and input schema.

steps.title: Resolved spec rides the /run wire
steps.description: customTools carries the CallbackToolSpec; toolCallback carries the shared callback endpoint and per-request authorization.

steps.title: Extension registers the tool natively
steps.description: The extension reads public metadata from AGENTA_TOOL_PUBLIC_SPECS and registers github__create_issue with Pi via pi.registerTool. Pi sees it as a native tool.

steps.title: Model calls the tool
steps.description: The model picks github__create_issue and supplies arguments; Pi emits an ACP tool_call update.

steps.title: Extension posts back to /tools/call
steps.description: On a local sandbox the extension runs on the runner host, so it reaches Agenta directly with the call_ref and the model's arguments in an OpenAI-style envelope.

steps.title: API executes the Composio action
steps.description: The router re-resolves the connection and runs the action through the Composio provider adapter.

steps.title: Result returned to the extension
steps.description: The result is already serialized as a JSON string in call.data.content; the runner hands it to the model verbatim.

scenarios.title: The same tool call, on a Daytona sandbox
scenarios.summary: With sandbox=daytona the in-sandbox process cannot reach Agenta directly. The runner creates a remote VM, pushes the login/extension/context files into it, and the same tool call is relayed through files instead of a direct HTTP call.

steps.title: Cold VM creation and file push
steps.description: The runner creates a Daytona VM (from an operator-run snapshot or the -full base image) and pushes the harness login, the Pi extension bundle, AGENTS.md, skills, and SYSTEM/APPEND_SYSTEM files over the Daytona filesystem API.

steps.title: Pi runs remotely; model calls the same gateway tool
steps.description: Inside the VM, the extension registers github__create_issue exactly as on local, but this time it cannot reach Agenta over the network.

steps.title: In-sandbox child writes a request file
steps.description: The extension writes <id>.req.json to the relay directory on the sandbox filesystem instead of calling out.

steps.title: Runner polls the sandbox filesystem
steps.description: sandboxRelayHost lists the relay dir via a remote `ls` exec (sandbox.runProcess), starting at a 300ms poll interval and backing off to 1500ms on an idle turn. It reads the new *.req.json.

steps.title: Runner executes the private spec in memory
steps.description: Same callback envelope and POST /tools/call as the local path; the only thing that changed is the transport hop between the sandbox and the runner.

steps.title: Runner writes the response file
steps.description: The runner writes <id>.res.json back to the sandbox filesystem (writeFsFile); the extension, which has been polling for it, reads the answer and hands it to Pi.

scenarios.title: A write tool hits an ask verdict (human-in-the-loop)
scenarios.summary: A Pi builtin write call (edit) is gated under the default allow_reads policy. The turn pauses, the browser shows an approval prompt, the user approves, and the resumed call is matched to the stored decision by a stable name+args anchor and consumed exactly once.

steps.title: Model calls a write builtin
steps.description: The model calls edit on a file. The grant list allowed it into the active tool set, but the relay still has to decide its permission.

steps.title: Extension hook reports a permission record
steps.description: The bundled extension's tool_call hook reports the builtin call over the relay as a permission record (kind: "permission"), because Pi has no separate harness-side settings gate.

steps.title: decide() resolves ask, no stored decision
steps.description: readOnlyHint for Edit is false. Under the run's allow_reads default, a non-read-hinted tool asks. No matching stored decision exists yet in this fresh conversation, so the verdict is pendingApproval.

steps.title: Turn pauses; one interaction_request fires
steps.description: onPendingApproval emits exactly one interaction_request(user_approval) event and the run stops with stopReason: "paused". No JSON-RPC result is returned for this call.

steps.title: Projected to the browser as an approval request
steps.description: The Vercel adapter maps interaction_request(user_approval) to a tool-approval-request stream part.

steps.title: User approves; next turn resends history plus the decision
steps.description: The browser sends a new /messages turn whose UIMessage history includes a tool_result for call_e771 with an {approved: true} envelope.

steps.title: Build the stored-decision map from replayed history
steps.description: extractApprovalDecisions scans tool_result blocks for the {approved} envelope and keys the decision by approvedCallKey(name, canonicalizedArgs), recovering name/args from the matching tool_call block.

steps.title: Harness re-issues the same call
steps.description: Pi (replaying the same conversation, cold) calls edit again with the identical arguments.

steps.title: Anchor match consumes the stored decision once
steps.description: The same name+canonicalized-args key matches. decide() returns allow from the stored decision and deletes it from the map, so a second identical call would ask again fresh.

scenarios.title: Trace propagation and the span tree
scenarios.summary: The service threads its own /invoke span's trace context into the /run request. Pi self-instruments its own span tree and writes a usage totals file; Claude has no extension, so the runner builds the same span shape from the ACP event stream. Both roll usage back onto the workflow span.

steps.title: Capture trace context from the live workflow span
steps.description: tracing.py's trace_context() reads the current workflow span's W3C traceparent and the operator's OTLP exporter config.

steps.title: Both blocks ride the /run request
steps.description: context (per-call propagation) and telemetry (operator config) are distinct fields, both filled from one service-side capture.

steps.title: Pi path: self-instrument as a child span
steps.description: The runner passes the traceparent to the extension as AGENTA_TRACEPARENT. The extension starts invoke_agent as a child of that span, so the whole Pi run joins the caller's trace with real per-call token counts.

steps.title: Span tree per turn
steps.description: Each turn nests a chat span (real provider usage) and one execute_tool span per tool call.

steps.title: Usage writeback on agent_end
steps.description: Pi reports no usage over ACP; the extension writes the run's real token/cost totals to a file (AGENTA_USAGE_OUT) since Pi's spans and the workflow span ship in separate OTel batches.

citations.section: Usage writeback: the one extra hop

steps.title: Runner reads the file and returns usage on the result
steps.description: The runner reads AGENTA_USAGE_OUT after the prompt finishes and includes it on the /run result.

steps.title: Claude path (contrast): no extension, event-stream tracing
steps.description: For a Claude run the runner has no in-process extension, so it subscribes to session/update ACP notifications and builds the identical span shape itself from the tool_call/tool_call_update/usage_update stream.

steps.title: Stamp usage on the workflow span
steps.description: tracing.py rolls the returned usage back onto the live workflow span. This is not added to the /invoke response body; it is recorded on the span only.
